channel.title: Breakfast With Boz Presented by Wahoo
channel.description: Breakfast with Boz is hosted by Ian Boswell, former cycling World Tour racer and now converted gravel racer. Breakfast with Boz brings you interviews with inspiring athletes and news from inside the race. It may not always be breakfast during the podcast but it will always be entertaining.
channel.copyright: 2020-2024, Breakfast With Boz Presented by Wahoo

item.title: State of Gravel with Kimo Seymour
item.description: Kimo Seymour is the President of Life Time Events and Media. As one of the largest stakeholders in off-road racing — through events such as UNBOUND Gravel and Leadville Trail 100 — Kimo and his team are at the forefront of shaping the off-road racing landscape. In this episode, Ian and Kimo discuss the introduction of new “super teams,” event safety, evolving rules, and how to keep the sport equitable for all. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See https://pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.
item.author: ian.boswell@wahoofitness.com (Wahoo Fitness)

item.title: Danni Shrosbree
item.description: Danni Shrosbree is a British professional gravel racer and former podium finisher at the UNBOUND 200. In this episode, Danni shares how she got into cycling, the growth of gravel racing across Europe and the UK, and how to get more young female riders into the sport she loves. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See https://pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.title: Dylan Johnson
item.description: Dylan Johnson is an American professional off-road racer known for setting trends in the endurance cycling space through his extensive, data-driven product testing. In this episode, he shares his passion for performance research alongside his return to competition after a near career-ending injury in 2025, when he was struck by a car just weeks before his favorite race, the UNBOUND 200. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See https://pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.title: Pete Stetina
item.description:
Pete Stetina has been racing bikes for nearly his entire life — and 2025 marks his final season chasing results. But before stepping away from the start line, he set his sights on one last ambitious goal: the Mt. Washington Hill Climb record.
In this episode of Breakfast with Boz, Pete joins Ian to share why Mt. Washington became his final big target, what stepping back from racing really looks like, and the story behind his new film, "Feeding the Rat." Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See https://pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.
item.author: ian.boswell@wahoofitness.com (Wahoo Fitness)

item.itunes:title: Matt Johnson
item.itunes:author: Wahoo Fitness
item.itunes:summary:
Matt Johnson knows what it means to go all-in. This fall, he ran 880 miles across Texas, from the Oklahoma state line all the way to Mexico — finishing in 19 days, 3 hours, and 32 minutes, and setting the record for the fastest time run across the state.
Along the way, Matt raised over $140,000 for ValorFit, an organization supporting veterans through fitness-based community programs.
In this episode of Breakfast with Boz, Matt joins Ian to talk about the physical and mental demands of the run, the why behind the mission, and the power of endurance to create meaningful impact.
Wahoo Presents Matt Johnson Film: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UkqgO3CdcxE&t=206s

item.title: Wahoo Training Metrics
item.description:
Training smarter starts with understanding your data.
All-new Wahoo cycling metrics are live in the Wahoo App! In this episode of Breakfast with Boz, Ian sits down with Alia Shafi and Mac Cassin from Wahoo’s Software and Sports Science team to unpack the philosophy behind these new insights and how they help you train with purpose.
From 4DP to training load, recovery trends, and more, tune in to learn how Wahoo’s latest tools make it easier to track your progress, focus your efforts, and build toward your 2026 goals. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See https://pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.title: Chris Cosentino
item.description:
“Healthy Elvis”

 

Almond Butter & Banana Toast

 

Yields 1 Portion

 

INGREDIENT AMOUNT UNIT

Multi grain batard bread, 1 slice about 1” thick 1 each

Almond butter 3 TBSP

Bananas, sliced 1 each

Toasted almonds, coarsely chopped 1 TBSP

Toasted Coconut flakes, unsweetened 2 tsp

Local honey ½ tsp

Creme fraiche or plain yogurt ½ tsp

Cocoa nibs, toasted ½ tsp

Flake salt pinch

 

METHOD

1. Toast the sliced bread

2. Spread the almond butter over the toast from end to end, making sure its evenly coated

so every bite gets almond butter.

3. Peel then slice the banana in rounds and lay them all over the almond butter covered

toast.

4. Using a fork, drizzle the honey over the bananas, this will also help the other items stick

to the toast.

5. Sprinkle the top of the toast with cocoa nibs, toasted almonds, & coconut flakes.

6. Using a spoon, drizzle creme fraiche or yogurt over the toast.

7. Finish with a sprinkle of flake salt.


Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.
item.author: ian.boswell@wahoofitness.com (Wahoo Fitness)

item.itunes:title: Chris Cosentino
item.itunes:author: Wahoo Fitness
item.itunes:summary:
World-renowned chef, restaurateur, and lifelong cyclist Chris Cosentino often blends his passion for food and sport together.
In this episode of Breakfast with Boz, Chris joins Ian to talk about his experience at the 2025 Tour de France and Tour de France Femmes, how the mindset from the kitchen translates to the bike, and why he continues to seek new challenges — from stove to saddle.
item.itunes:subtitle:
World-renowned chef, restaurateur, and lifelong cyclist Chris Cosentino often blends his passion for food and sport together.
In this episode of Breakfast with Boz, Chris joins Ian to talk about his experience at the 2025 Tour de France and Tour de France Femmes, how the mindset from the kitchen translates to the bike, and why he continues to seek new challenges — from stove to saddle.

item.title: Geoff Proctor
item.description:
Geoff Proctor has spent over three decades in cyclocross — as a racer, coach, and former USAC National Cyclocross Director. He’s also the founder of the Euro Cross Academy, helping develop the next generation of champions.
In this episode of Breakfast with Boz, Geoff joins Ian to discuss the history and growth of cyclocross, athlete development pathways in the U.S. and abroad, and the lessons learned from decades shaping the sport, mentoring riders from local races to world championship podiums.
Euro Cross Academy Video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wpc3Vvu0Css Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See https://pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.title: Amy Perryman
item.description:
Amy Perryman - British pro cyclocross racer and Wahoo ambassador - is setting her sights on the upcoming CX season both at home in England and abroad in Europe.
Join Amy and Ian on this episode of Breakfast with Boz for a chat about the growth and potential of women’s cycling, Amy's favorite mid-ride snacks, and how she keeps motivated to stay focused and fast on the bike. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See https://pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.title: Andric Queen-Booker
item.description: Andric Queen-Booker is a filmmaker, director, and Wahoo ambassador who's built friendships and community wherever he's ridden. In this episode of Breakfast with Boz, Ian sits down with Andric to talk about what cycling means to him, the power of community, and how the bike is the vehicle for connecting people across borders and cultures. item.title: David Roche
item.description:
Two-time Leadville 100 champion and course record holder, David Roche has reached the pinnacle of ultra running, yet it's his scientific approach to training, racing, and coaching is what's helped him push beyond skill.
In this episode, David sits down with Ian to talk about his record-setting runs, the triumph of racing Western States, and how his passion for the sport is helping redefine what's possible. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See https://pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.title: Gareth Joyce
item.description: Wahoo's CEO Gareth Joyce isn’t just leading the charge of the brand — he’s living the endurance athlete lifestyle, too. In this episode of Breakfast with Boz, Gareth sits down with Ian after a ride in Vermont to talk about his path to Wahoo, the lessons learned from his time at Delta Airlines and Mercedes-Benz, and his vision for driving the brand that builds the better athlete in all of us. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See https://pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.title: Magdeleine Vallieres
item.description: Ahead of her fourth Tour de France Femmes avec Zwift appearance, Ian is joined by EF Education-Oatly's Magdeleine Vallieres for a chat on everything from racing the biggest Grand Tour of the year to her approach this season, and what makes EF Pro Cycling the tight-knit squad they've become at this year's race. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See https://pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.title: Luke Rowe
item.description:
Luke Rowe knows what's needed to take on the Tour de France. With eight appearances (five of which were on winning teams), the former pro-turned-Sports Director has logged more miles than most at the grandest of the Grand Tours.
In this episode of Breakfast with Boz, Luke joins Ian to share his stories from inside the peloton, insights on what makes a winning team successful, predictions on this year's race, and behind-the-scenes access to his current team, Decathlon-AG2R. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See https://pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.title: Elle Purrier St. Pierre
item.description:
Elle Purrier St. Pierre is an Olympic medalist, indoor track world champion, dairy farmer, and mother of two — balancing it all from her home state of Vermont.
In this episode of Breakfast with Boz, join the two Vermonters for a chat on chasing world-class competition while living in the Green Mountain State, lessons they've learned from rural living, and the forces driving Elle to be the best version of herself. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See https://pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.title: Emma Pallant-Browne
item.description:
Emma Pallant-Browne is no stranger to competing at the world's biggest races, with multiple IRONMAN 70.3 World Championship podiums – and wins – to her name. But now, she’s stepping into a brand-new chapter: pregnancy.
In this episode of Breakfast with Boz, Emma joins Ian to talk about how she’s stayed active while expecting her first child, the support she’s felt from the endurance community, and her mindset on returning to elite racing post-pregnancy. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See https://pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.title: Unbound 2025
item.description: UNBOUND 2025 was one for the books — and Ian was in the thick of it. Fresh off a Top 10 finish, Boz takes us through the energy of this year’s event — from the expo to the start line, to everything in between. Hear from Wahooligan athletes, ambassadors, racers, and fans with an on-the-ground look at what makes UNBOUND the pinnacle of what gravel racing has become. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. item.title: Inside the Women's Peloton with Dominique Powers Ep. 3 The Riders
item.description:
In the third and final episode of the Wahoo podcast takeover, Dominique Powers brings us into the heart of the women’s pro peloton — through the voices of the riders themselves.
Hear from Lidl-Trek's Lucinda Brand on the differences between racing cyclocross and road. Human Powered Health's Lily Williams shares what it’s like to balance Olympic-level goals while being a dedicated teammate, and Letizia Borghesi on how racing with EF Education-Oatly has made her career as a pro even more meaningful. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See https://pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.
item.author: ian.boswell@wahoofitness.com (Wahoo Fitness)

item.itunes:author: Wahoo Fitness
item.itunes:summary:
In the third and final episode of the Wahoo podcast takeover, Dominique Powers brings us into the heart of the women’s pro peloton — through the voices of the riders themselves.
Hear from Lidl-Trek's Lucinda Brand on the differences between racing cyclocross and road. Human Powered Health's Lily Williams shares what it’s like to balance Olympic-level goals while being a dedicated teammate, and Letizia Borghesi on how racing with EF Education-Oatly has made her career as a pro even more meaningful.

Human Powered Health's Lily Williams shares what it’s like to balance Olympic-level goals while being a dedicated teammate, and Letizia Borghesi on how racing with EF Education-Oatly has made her career as a pro even more meaningful.

item.title: FireFlies Patagonia 2025
item.description:
Fireflies Patagonia is a cycling epic through some of Chile's most sought-after routes — all in support of children's cancer clinics.
In this episode of Breakfast with Boz, join Ian for daily recaps, reflections, interviews, and insights into the 2025 trip and just what makes Fireflies so special. item.title: Phil Burt on Indoor Riding Fit
item.description:
World-renowned bike fitter and physiotherapist Phil Burt has dialed bikes for riders ranging from the pros at Team Sky, to British Cycling Olympians, and even Tour de France legends.
In this episode of Breakfast with Boz, Phil shares valuable insights on the importance of your indoor bike fit and why it's crucial for injury prevention and performance. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See https://pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.title: Run Streak with Hella Sidibe
item.description:
Runner and former soccer pro Hellah Sidibe has been on a non-stop run streak every day since May 15, 2017. His only criteria: the run has to be at least 2 miles.
Join Boz as he chats with Hellah about what it takes to stay motivated, his goals for the upcoming year, and how he maintains the run streak that transformed his life. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See https://pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.title: Ride Streak with Colin Gay
item.description: What does it take to ride 10,000 days in a row? Colin Gay is 10 years and 3,700 rides into finding that out. Now almost halfway into a 27-year challenge with over 6,000 rides still ahead, Colin reflects on the highs, lows, and lessons learned so far as he takes on this extraordinary challenge on this episode of Breakfast with Boz. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See https://pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.title: Bond Almand
item.description:
This year, ultra-cyclist Bond Almand set the world record for the fastest known time on the Pan American Highway, a 19,000-mile journey by bike from Alaska to the Southernmost city in Argentina.
Now after the journey, Bond and Ian sit down for a chat on everything from endurance, to ambition, goals for the future, and the mindset it takes to complete a feat of this caliber. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See https://pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.title: Past, Present, and Future of Human Powered Health
item.description:
In this special episode of Breakfast with Boz, Ian is joined by Charles and riders Ruth Edwards and Lily Williams for an inside look at the team’s journey, their current state as a UCI World Tour team, and their exciting vision for the future.
Tune in to hear more about the wins and challenges of competing at the highest level and the inspiration behind creating a stronger, more inclusive cycling community for riders and fans everywhere! item.title: Eric Lagerstrom
item.description:
Eric Lagerstrom embodies the multi-sport lifestyle, and while you may have seen him competing in some of the world's biggest triathlons, watched his YouTube videos, or listened to his podcast, you'll find his love of exploration and adventure outdoors is extremely apparent.
Now with his first ultra-trail win under his belt, Eric is joined by Ian for the latest episode of Breakfast with Boz for a chat about the current state of professional triathlon, his plans for competition in the new year, and his goals for his career moving forward. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See https://pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.title: Geoff Burns on Running Shoe Evolution
item.description: Researcher, engineer, Sports Science PhD, Ultra-marathoner and Physiologist for Team USA and USOPC — Geoff Burns knows his stuff when it comes to running shoes. Now, as Ian begins his foray into marathoning with his first race coming this year, he's joined by Geoff for a deep dive into the history of running shoes and insight on how the modern super shoe maximizes performance and helps avoid injury. item.title: Sarah Lange
item.description:
While she's still relatively new to the world of bike racing, Velocio // Exploro's Sarah Lange has risen the ranks at some of the nation's biggest off-road events — this year Sarah came in fifth at UNBOUND and Leadville respectively.
However, racing bikes isn't her only discipline, when she's not riding, you'll find Sarah working as a Clinical Dietitian, helping her clients unlock their full potential.
Tune in to hear more from Sarah about everything from being a newcomer in the sport, to her rapid rise in gravel, and her insights into modern sports nutrition. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See https://pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.description: Adventure cyclist Lael Wilcox has just wrapped up an incredible tour around the world on her bike, taking the current record and completing the trip in a whopping 108 days 12 hours and 12 minutes. item.title: Clara Brown
item.description:
From piloting planes to Paralympic podiums and World Championships, Clara Brown is no stranger to flying high.
Yet that wasn't always the case. When Clara was 12, she suffered a traumatic spinal cord injury that left her paralyzed from the neck down.
Tune into the latest episode of Breakfast with Boz to hear from Clara about discovering cycling, competing in Paris, and what it's like training on the East Coast. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See https://pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.itunes:title: Team Amani Legacy and Action
item.itunes:author: Wahoo Fitness
item.itunes:summary: This episode with Team Amani was recorded last August a few days prior to the Vermont Overland, a race in which John Kariuki won and where Sule Kangangi tragically lost his life. The Amani riders will carry on the legacy that Sule so passionately dreamed about and made possible for his fellow East African riders and friends.
item.itunes:subtitle: This episode with Team Amani was recorded last August a few days prior to the Vermont Overland, a race in which John Kariuki won and where Sule Kangangi tragically lost his life. The Amani riders will carry on the legacy that Sule so passionately dreamed about and made possible for his fellow East African riders and friends.

item.title: Concussion in Cycling
item.description: Crashes happen, and unfortunately, concussions can happen, too. item.title: Holiday Health and Nutrition
item.description:
Recipe courtesy of Chris Cosentino

Roasted Brussels Sprouts & Black garlic Bagna Cauda

Let’s be honest a lot of the experiences of Brussels Sprouts have been lackluster at best. This one takes this not so loved vegetable and turns it into the star of the show.

Serving size 4-6

1 Brussels Sprout Stalk

2 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil

Kosher Salt

Black pepper, fresh ground

Aleppo chili

For dipping: Black Garlic Bagna Cauda (see recipe below)

Bring a tall pot of salted water to boil, while waiting for the pot of water to boil remove any ugly leaves or empty stems still attached to the stalk. Pre heat the oven to 500 Degree’s, once the water is boiling stick the bottom end of the stalk in and let blanch for 5 minutes, be sure to turn it as some of the sprouts will be just above the water. Repeat this same process for the top half of the stalk be sure to test the sprouts with a squeeze, they will give a bit and feel tender but not mushy.  Transfer the stalk to a sheet pan with a piece of parchment paper or nonstick pad, drizzle the stalk with extra virgin olive oil while still warm then season with the salt, Aleppo & fresh ground black pepper.

Place in the pre heated oven for 15 minutes rotating the stalk to get even color and crispiness all over the stalk. You will know it’s done; the aroma will be amazing and the Brussels Sprouts with be caramelized and be crispy. Remove from the oven and transfer to a serving tray, set on the table a few dipping items for the Sprouts then carve it like a turkey making the Sprout Stalk the star.

Black Garlic Bagna Cauda

Serves 10

4 each salted anchovy filets

¼ [one quarter] cup white anchovy fillets

¼ cup lemon juice

Micro plane Zest from 1 lemon

6 cloves black garlic

1 cup extra virgin olive oil

½  cup olive oil

freshly ground black pepper

Kosher salt


Soak the salted anchovies in cold water to remove the excess salt and allow for easier filleting.  Fillet all of the anchovies, discarding the bones and guts. In a blender combine all the ingredients and blend until smooth. Adjust the seasoning with salt and pepper to taste. Then serve warm with the roasted Brussels Sprouts stalk.


 


Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.
item.author: ian.boswell@wahoofitness.com (Wahoo Fitness)

item.title: Bikepacking for Conservation: Lael Wilcox
item.description:
This week Lael Wilcox joins us on Breakfast with Boz to share her experience of bikepacking a newly created route with Bikepacking.com and Conservation International. Ruta Chingaza is a 420km bike route around the Paramos of Colombia which provides 70% of the fresh drinking water for Bogota Colombia. Be sure to also check out the Ruta Chingaza film that coincides with this podcast on Wahoo's YouTube channel.
View the film here: https://youtu.be/xDq-m_Pi0VY Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See https://pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.title: Life After Racing: Marcel Kittel and Peter Kennaugh
item.description: Professional athletes are some of the most dedicated and focused individuals out there. They dream, sacrifice, and work towards continual improvement in both body and mind. At some point, the career of every athlete comes to an end. How do they make that choice, is that choice made for them and what values do they take away from a career and quest of seeking perfection. This week we hear from Marcel Kittel and Peter Kennaugh on how they came to the decision to stop their careers and how they are adapting to life after racing. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See https://pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.
item.author: ian.boswell@wahoofitness.com (Wahoo Fitness)

This week we hear from Marcel Kittel and Peter Kennaugh on how they came to the decision to stop their careers and how they are adapting to life after racing.

At some point, the career of every athlete comes to an end. How do they make that choice, is that choice made for them and what values do they take away from a career and quest of seeking perfection. This week we hear from Marcel Kittel and Peter Kennaugh on how they came to the decision to stop their careers and how they are adapting to life after racing.
item.itunes:keywords: ineos cycling, peter kennaugh, pro cycling, wahoo, cycling, wahoo fitness, team sky, breakfast with boz, ian boswell, marcel kittel

item.title: The Growth of Women's Cycling
item.description:
While the Tour de France was taking place earlier this month, the most prestigious women's stage race, The Giro Rosa, was happening in Italy. The race was not televised and did not receive the attention it deserved, so this week we catch up with Lizzy Banks and Lisa Brennauer to talk about the race, but more importantly the growth of women's cycling.

item.title: The Final Week - The Tour de France
item.description:
In this episode of Breakfast with Boz we connect with riders at the Tour de France to hear about how their race is going. Sep Kuss is riding his first Tour this year in support of the Yellow jersey wearer Primoz Roglich while Connor Swift is riding his first grand tour in support of Nairo Quintana. Hear from the rookies and the veterans on this episode. We also welcome 14 time Tour de France stage winner, Marcel Kittel, to find out who he thinks is the fastest sprinter in this week's race.
Guests include: Sep Kuss, Connor Swift, Tejay Van Garderen, Nico Roche, and George Bennett.

item.title: Why We Ride With Joe Cruz
item.description: We all love to ride, yet why do we push, challenge, and test ourselves both physically and mentally? This week I dive into those questions and try to get some answers. I am joined by bike packing legend and philosophy professor Joe Cruz and a fellow bike packer Karen Yung. Join us as we go down the wormhole of understanding WHY. item.title: The South Downs Way
item.description:
Last summer 4 friends took on the South Downs Way - an ancient 100-mile ridgeline that spans the southeast coast of England. Starting in Winchester and finishing in East Bourne the historic chalk path has over 3500m of climbing and not a car in sight, just rolling steep hills as far as the eye can see.
The South Downs was the training ground of UK cycling legend Sean Yates, the third UK rider to wear the Yellow Jersey. Now his son Liam Yates carries on the tradition, joined by mates Neil Philips, Sophie Edmondson, and Anna Mcleod.
In these strange times of isolation, this short film is a reminder to all of the simple joys of cycling with friends. A joy that for the time being must remain a memory. Please do not travel to the South Downs during this period of lockdown, it is a beautiful ancient land and it isn't going anywhere. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See https://pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.
item.author: ian.boswell@wahoofitness.com (Anna Mcleod, Liam Yates, Ian Boswell)

Starting in Winchester and finishing in East Bourne the historic chalk path has over 3500m of climbing and not a car in sight, just rolling steep hills as far as the eye can see.
The South Downs was the training ground of UK cycling legend Sean Yates, the third UK rider to wear the Yellow Jersey. Now his son Liam Yates carries on the tradition, joined by mates Neil Philips, Sophie Edmondson, and Anna Mcleod.
In these strange times of isolation, this short film is a reminder to all of the simple joys of cycling with friends. A joy that for the time being must remain a memory. Please do not travel to the South Downs during this period of lockdown, it is a beautiful ancient land and it isn't going anywhere.
item.itunes:keywords: bikepacking, cycling, liam yates, wahoo fitness, ian boswell, the south downs way, gravel bike

item.title: Yuri Hauswald - Gandalf of Gravel
item.description: Known as the Gandalf of Gravel, Yuri Hauswald is a legend of the cycling community. From mountain bike racing in the mid 90's to winning Dirty Kanza in 2015, Yuri is a rider we can all learn something from. Hear from Yuri on this week's episode of Breakfast with Boz served by Wahoo. item.title: Classics Edition with Fabian Cancellara
item.description:
In this episode of Breakfast with Boz we catch up with classics legend Fabian Cancellara and Belgian rider Oliver Naesen. The Classics have long been a fan and rider favorite each spring with events like Paris Roubaix and Flanders. Fabian shares what he is doing now and reflects on some of his most fond memories of racing. Oliver discusses the impact of Covid19 in Belgium and his Classics goals.
Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See https://pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.title: Detours with Sarah Sturm
item.description:
"You have to have grit and mental toughness because it is so brutal out there."
Sarah Sturm has been paving her own way since childhood. She entered the sport of cycling while in college in Durango, Colorado. Cycling was her anchor in college, providing her a community that was missing since her move from home in Albuquerque, New Mexico.
In this episode, we chat with Sarah about community, mental health and no longer being a dark horse on the start line. Sarah has always focused on her mental health and made career decisions that have made her happy and healthy.
Tune in next week for our Classics episode. We catch up with legend Fabian Cancellara and relive some of his fondest Classics memories.
Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See https://pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.
item.author: ian.boswell@wahoofitness.com (Wahoo Fitness)

item.title: Detours with Coach Neal Henderson
item.description:
In this episode, Ian connects with Wahoo Sports Science coach Neal Henderson to discuss training indoors during these times. With the shift to indoors for most athletes and society as a whole we wanted to provide athletes some tools to stay healthy, fit and happy. Neal is a legendary coach who has worked with athletes at all levels of endurance sport and currently manages the newly launched Sports Science division at Wahoo.
Ian then chats with the Ultra Cyclist himself, Mr. Jack Thompson who is currently on lockdown in Girona, Spain. Jack discusses reevaluating goals, setting new ones and managing what you can manage. He is encouraging to listen to and will gives a unique perspective. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See https://pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.
item.author: ian.boswell@wahoofitness.com (Ian Boswell, Jack Thompson, Neal Henderson)

item.title: Corona Detours
item.description:
With the Covid19 pandemic being front and center in most people's lives right now we wanted to speak to the larger cycling community about the impact this global crisis is having. In this episode, Ian catches up with world tour riders Larry Warbasse and Tiffany Cromwell to see how they are handling things in Europe then speaks with Wahoo Frontiers athlete Amity Rockwell and race/ ride director Heidi Myers from Rasputitsa.
While riding and racing a bike is currently not nearly as important as the life-saving measures that are being taken to avoid this pandemic from becoming something larger than it currently is, riding brings joy to our lives. Having joy in a time like this is good for humanity.
Please stay safe, stay healthy, stay happy. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See https://pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.
item.author: ian.boswell@wahoofitness.com (Larry Warbasse, Heidi Meyers, Ian Boswell, Tiffany Cromwell, Amity Rockwell)

item.title: Pete Stetina - Ruining Gravel?
item.description: In this episode of Breakfast with Boz Ian speaks with Pete Stetina about his gravel racing plans in 2020 after winning the 2019 edition of Belgian Waffle Ride. Ian also gives us an update on world tour racing, or the lack of racing and how the Covid 19 pandemic is affecting racing around the world. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See https://pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.itunes:title: Bonus Episode - Ian Boswell
item.itunes:author: Ian Boswell
item.itunes:summary:
In this "half" episode of the Breakfast with Boz podcast Ian updates us on what he has been up to since joining the Wahoo team and what his race/ event calendar will be for 2020.
These "half" episodes will be released between each longer episode and will be a short update on what has been going on and what is to come. These bite-size episodes are entertaining and informative and should not be missed.
